expectations.text: Has at least 4 distinct priority levels or numbered sections
expectations.evidence: Four Priority sections: Priority 1 (Quick Defensive Fixes), Priority 2 (Unit Tests), Priority 3 (Refactor distill_advanced_bert.py), Priority 4 (Future Work)

expectations.text: Covers the defensive fixes: minilm_relation_heads validation, attention check, __init__.py, .gitignore
expectations.evidence: Priority 1 items 1a-1d cover all four: minilm_relation_heads validation, attention output validation, __init__.py creation, and .gitignore addition

expectations.text: Covers unit tests as a missing item
expectations.evidence: Priority 2 section with table of 11 functions to test, each with specific verification criteria

expectations.text: Covers the BERT refactor including code duplication and MiniLMv2 forward pass
expectations.evidence: Priority 3 items 3a and 3b: 8 replacement items with exact line ranges and import paths, plus MiniLMv2 duplicate forward pass fix with instructions to follow _qwen_last_q pattern

expectations.text: Covers future features from README (GKD, sequence-level, DPO, TAKD, LoRA or similar)
expectations.evidence: Priority 4 items 4c-4g explicitly list: GKD, Sequence-level distillation, DPO/Preference distillation, TAKD, LoRA on student

expectations.text: Items are ordered by priority: quick fixes first, future features last
expectations.evidence: Clear P1→P2→P3→P4 ordering with explicit 'Blockers/Dependencies' section confirming: 'Priority 1 items have no dependencies — can be done in any order'

expectations.text: References specific file paths in the advanced/ folder

suggestions.reason: File paths are relative (e.g., 'distill_advanced.py') rather than absolute (e.g., 'E:\Distillation\advanced\distill_advanced.py'). The with-skill version used absolute paths which are more actionable. Minor difference, not worth failing.

eval_feedback.overall: Surprisingly strong baseline — the without_skill run produced a checklist nearly as comprehensive as the with_skill version. The main difference is the with_skill version includes dependency/blocker analysis and absolute paths. This eval may not strongly discriminate skill quality for checklist-style requests since both approaches produce similar outputs when given access to the same codebase.
